The paper explores two embedding techniques: word embeddings and second-order co-occurrence vectors. Word embeddings are a type of distributional model that uses a vector space model to represent words as vectors in a high-dimensional space. Second-order co-occurrence vectors are created by taking the co-occurrence of two words in a corpus.
